sn15.9:1.1: Near Sāvatthī. There the Blessed One said:
sn15.9:1.2: “From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on.
sn15.9:1.3: A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on.
sn15.9:1.4: Just as a stick thrown up in the air lands sometimes on its base, sometimes on its side, sometimes on its tip;
sn15.9:1.5: in the same way, beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving, transmigrating & wandering on, sometimes go from this world to another world, sometimes come from another world to this.
sn15.9:1.7: From an inconceivable beginning comes the wandering-on. A beginning point is not discernible, though beings hindered by ignorance and fettered by craving are transmigrating & wandering on. Long have you thus experienced stress, experienced pain, experienced loss, swelling the cemeteries—enough to become disenchanted with all fabricated things, enough to become dispassionate,
